suggestions.title: R-key refresh does not clear paused polling state
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 896: the keyboard shortcut calls syncFromGitHub() directly instead of onResume(). onResume() sets pollingPaused to false AND updates lastActivity.current — syncFromGitHub() does neither. So pressing R while paused triggers one data fetch, but the PAUSED banner stays visible and the 15s inactivity check re-parks polling immediately. Fix: replace `syncFromGitHub()` with `onResume()` in the keydown handler.

suggestions.title: Workers transitioning to 'done' status disappear silently with no celebration
suggestions.description: activeWorkers filters on status === 'working' | 'idle' | 'error' (App.jsx line 964). Workers that go directly working→done skip the justShipped check entirely (which only fires for working→idle, line 1037). They fall into the 'disappeared' branch: 400ms fade + 'depart' door event. No confetti, no completion toast, no worker-specific celebration line. The whole WORKER_COMPLETION_LINES map is useless for done-status workers.

suggestions.title: Mobile: pinned hover card has no visible dismiss control
suggestions.description: On touch devices, clicking a character pins the hover card (Office.jsx line 749). Dismissal relies on clicking outside the .char element (line 685-689) — but there is no visible X / close button inside the hover card (lines 823-843). Touch users can be stuck with the card open until they intuit the 'tap elsewhere' interaction. At minimum, add a small close button inside the card that only renders when pinnedCard is true.

suggestions.title: Footer quote counter hides on the last item in the cycle
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1473: `quoteBonus > 0 && quoteBonus < BONUS_TAGLINES.length` — when quoteBonus reaches BONUS_TAGLINES.length (8), the counter is hidden because 8 < 8 is false. The last tagline is shown without its counter. The onClick wraps quoteBonus back to 1 on the next click so users never see '8/8'. Fix: change condition to `quoteBonus > 0 && quoteBonus <= BONUS_TAGLINES.length`.

suggestions.title: Worker cards not keyboard-navigable via Tab
suggestions.description: WorkerCard renders with tabIndex={-1} (App.jsx line 321). Cards cannot receive focus via Tab — only the info button inside them is reachable. Screen-reader and keyboard-only users can't navigate between cards, only into individual controls inside them. The card acts as an article/region and warrants a tabIndex={0} with appropriate focus styling, or at least a visible landmark heading per card.

suggestions.title: Office.css is still 2411 lines — split into feature-scoped modules
suggestions.description: Office.css remains at exactly 2411 lines, unchanged from last review. Suggested modules: characters.css, desks.css, animations.css, widgets.css (clock, whiteboard, ficus), office-floor.css. The file mixes global variables, component scopes, keyframes, and responsive breakpoints in a single flat file. This is a maintenance liability as the feature surface grows.

suggestions.title: Sky gradient 2h window desync — claimed fixed, still present
suggestions.description: WindowElement (Office.jsx lines 866-875): getGradient returns the sunset gradient for h >= 17 && h <= 21 (5 hours), but isGoldenHour is h >= 17 && h <= 19 (3 hours). Hours 20 and 21 display the warm sunset gradient with NO golden-hour clouds — the sky literally has a sunset glow with empty air. This was listed as fixed in the last commit but the code is unchanged. isDaytime cutoff at 16 also means 17:00 transitions sharply with no overlap handling.

suggestions.title: isTouchDevice evaluated at module load — breaks hybrid devices
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 692: `const isTouchDevice = typeof window !== 'undefined' && window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)').matches` is a module-level constant. On Surface Pro / iPad keyboard users, this is evaluated once at page load and stays stale for the session. The Office.jsx Character component (line 748) already correctly uses `window.matchMedia('(hover: none)').matches` inside handleClick for dynamic evaluation. App.jsx should do the same — read the media query at call-time, not at module load.

suggestions.title: getRelativeTime returns 'just now' for future-dated timestamps
suggestions.description: time.js line 6: `if (mins < 1) return 'just now'` — diff is computed as Date.now() - timestamp. If timestamp is in the future, diff is negative, mins rounds to 0 or a negative value that still hits < 1. A future-dated activity log entry will show 'just now' indefinitely instead of surfacing the data anomaly. Fix: add a guard `if (diff < 0) return 'just now'` separately, and add a comment explaining the intended behavior.

suggestions.title: ErrorBoundary retry button uses no design system styles
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 17-20: the ErrorBoundary fallback UI uses raw inline styles (color: #f87171, marginLeft: 1rem). The rest of the app uses CSS classes from App.css (btn, btn-sm, btn-ghost etc.) and CSS custom properties (var(--surface2), var(--text), var(--border)). The error screen will look visually inconsistent, especially in light mode where hardcoded dark colors break. Use design system classes and variables.

suggestions.title: Vibe history written to localStorage on every teamVibe.key change including initial mount
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1113-1119: the vibe history append runs unconditionally inside `useEffect([teamVibe.key])`. The isInitialMount guard on line 1079 only protects the streak counter, not the history write. On every cold load, the current vibe is immediately pushed to history before any real vibe transition has occurred. Wrap the history push in the same `!isInitialMount` check used for streak.

suggestions.title: Bloberto speech bubble overflows tab bar on mobile
suggestions.description: Confirmed in screenshot: on 375px viewport the speech bubble positioned above Bloberto clips over the tab toggle bar ('Office' / 'Dashboard'). The bubble uses absolute positioning relative to the character element, with no viewport-aware clamping. On small screens where Bloberto is near the top of the office-floor, the bubble can escape the container bounds. Needs a max-width clamp and translateX adjustment to keep it within the office-floor boundary.

verified_fixes: JSON.parse on VIBE_HISTORY_KEY now wrapped in try-catch (App.jsx lines 1114-1115) — corrupted localStorage no longer throws
SVG glow filter ID in ConferenceTable now uses useId() (Office.jsx line 908) — no more hardcoded id collision risk across multiple mounted instances
ErrorBoundary contradictory message removed — fallback now shows a clean 'Something went wrong.' with a functional Retry button
praise: The ping reaction system (PING_REACTIONS map + handleCharClick) is genuinely charming — every worker has 5 personality-correct one-liners and the pinged animation + speech bubble chain works cleanly
Completion celebration is properly per-worker with staggered confetti (2.6s apart), role-colored particles, and worker-specific WORKER_COMPLETION_LINES text — well above the bar for a dashboard tool
ConferenceTable now correctly uses useId() for SVG filter IDs — good catch in the last commit
The polling visibility-change handler is consolidated into a single effect with correct interval reset, title animation, and resume-on-return behavior — no more duplicate handlers
VibeSparkline with ranked y-positions and per-vibe colors is a nice touch — sparkline-legend pills with last-3 history are readable and non-intrusive
WindowElement's getHour tick is aligned to the next minute boundary, not a raw setInterval — clean approach that avoids drift
Lone survivor labels per-worker (LONE_SURVIVOR_WORKER_OVERRIDES) with vibe-specific overrides is the kind of detail that makes the app feel alive
ficus plant lifecycle (seedling → blooming → grief on streak break) with toast notification is delightful and mechanically sound
